Re:
Request for Written Advice
Gentlemen:
This office represents the Washington Township Hospital
District of Alameda County, California.
We have been asked to seek
your advice
as to whether or not the Washington Hospital Authority
of Alameda County. California
is
a "local government agency" within
the meaning of Government Code Section 62041
and whether or not
its
separate Board of Directors
are “public officials" within the meaning
of Government Code Sections
82048 and 87100 due
to the limited capacity
in which they presently function.
on April 1, 1970 the Washington Township Hospital District
and the County of Alameda entered into a joint exercise of powers agree-
ment creating a separate entity, the Washington Hospital Authority.
(See Exhibit
"A")
The Board of Directors of said Authority is composed
of
5 members, four appointed by the District and one appointed by the
County.
No member can be
a member of the Board of Directors of the
District, or a member of the Board of Supervisors of the County.
The
appointments are for staggered terms of four yearsi
The Authority was created to provide
a funding vehicle
for constructing additions to and expansion of the existing Washington
Hospital, owned and operated by the District.
The expansion of the
hospital was approved by the electors of the District by a 72% majority.
The Authority
leased the hospital sites
and facilities
from the District
{See Exhibit B Site Leaseﬁ
,
issued Revenue Bonds
in
the amount of $9,700,000,
constructed the imgrovements thereon
and
leased the hospital
site,
facilities and improvements back to the
District for an annual rental sufficient to pay principal and. interest
on the Bonds and all
administrative costs.
(See Exhibit C Project
Lease!


Fair Political r'
:tices commission
5“
February 25, 1977
Page 2
Re:
Request for Written Advice
During the construction period the Authority was required
to employ the District as its Agent for construction of the impraver
ments in accordance with the plans and specifications approved by
the District.
The Authority had no staff personnel of its own. All
staff functions were performed by District staff pcrsonnel.
The
issuance of the Bonds, the approval of the Corporate Trustee, the
Resolution of issuance, the site and Project Leases, all contracts
entered into and all monies disbursed were approved by the District
prior to approval of the Authority.
The proceeds from the sale of the Authority Bonds were
deposited with the Bank of America National Trust and savings
Association. corporate Trustee in trust, pursuant to the Resolution
of Issuance Asee Exhibit D).
All other revenues including rentals
of the Authority are paid directly to the Corporate Trustee.
The
corporate Trustee disbursed all Authority funds for the construction
of the improvements and now disburses all funds of the Authority
for the payment of principal and interest directly to the bondholders.
At no time has the Authority or will it in the future directly re-
ceive or disburse funds on its own.
On March a, 1974 the construction of said hospital implover
ments were accepted as completed by the District as Agent for the
Authority.
Pursuant to the project
Lease, the District commenced
payments of rent to the corporate Trustee on behalf of the Authority.
in accordance with the terms of the construction contracts all costs
and expenses of said improvements were paid.
During the course of
construction the District had physical possession of the entire pre-
mises and operated and maintained the hospital.
After construction
and at the present time the Distiict has full use and benefit of the
entire premises and facilities and pursuant to the Project Lease is
obligated to maintain and operate the hospital, site and facilities,
including fire and extended coverage, liability, earthquake and
rental income insurance at its own costs.
Also, pursuant to the Project Lease, the District each
year must take necessary action to include all rental payments in
its budget and make necessary appropriations therefor to service the
hands.
now that the preject is complete the Authority Board of
Directors meets annually to review the District's approved budget
and reviews the audit report of the Authority by an independent
Certified Public Accountant.


Fair Political [uctices Commission
C
February 25, 1977
Page 3
Re:
Request for Written Advice
until the expiration of the Leases and payment of the
Revenue Bonds in full, which is projected to occur in 1997, the
District carries the
full burden of operating and maintaining the
hospital site and facilities and the payment of the Revenue Bonds.
Although the Authority is in the position of sublessor
anti must review from time to time the
financial status of the Trust
Accounts and the District's budget it would EPPEar the Authority
functions in a very limited capacity and is not making the kind of
governmental decisions contemplated by chapter 7 of the Political
Reform Act of 1974.
There is no question the District must comply with the
Act
and has
in
fact adopted its own Conflict of Interest Caﬂe more
restrictive than the law requires, which Code is presently being
reviewed by Alameda County.
Very truly yours,
MARVIN

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Washington Hospital Authority is a 'local government agency' under Government Code Section 62041 and whether its Board members are 'public officials' under Government Code Sections 82048 and 87100.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC response indicates that the issues raised are identical to those in the Samuel Siegel matter (Opinion Request 75-054) and that the Commission's determination in that case will govern staff advice regarding the Washington Hospital Authority. A second hearing was scheduled for early April 1977, and the requester was placed on the mailing list for the Siegel opinion.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document appears to contain both the FPPC response letter (dated March 4, 1977) and the incoming request letter (dated February 25, 1977). The response letter is brief and defers substantive analysis to a pending Commission opinion. No formal question or conclusion sections are present in typical FPPC advice letter format. OCR quality affects confidence level.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Washington Hospital Authority is a 'local government agency' under Government Code Section 62041 and whether its Board members are 'public officials' under Government Code Sections 82048 and 87100.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC response indicates that the issues raised are identical to those in the Samuel Siegel matter (Opinion Request 75-054) and that the Commission's determination in that case will govern staff advice regarding the Washington Hospital Authority. A second hearing was scheduled for early April 1977, and the requester was placed on the mailing list for the Siegel opinion.

embedding.summary: An FPPC staff response regarding whether the Washington Hospital Authority, a joint powers entity created to finance hospital improvements, qualifies as a local government agency and whether its directors are public officials under the Political Reform Act. The response defers to a pending Commission opinion on an identical legal issue.
